## What is Environmental DNA Metabarcoding?

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## [Barcoding]{style="color:#02C39A"} + [Meta]{style="color:#E8A020"}

**Barcoding** = species ID via a genetic marker sequence  
**Meta** = all together (all organisms at once)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## Choosing the right barcode

- Defines which organisms can be identified
- Trade-off: broad groups vs species-level precision
- Common markers: 12S, 16S, 18S, COI, ITS
:::

:::

::::

::: notes
Environmental DNA is any DNA collected from an environmental sample without first isolating target organisms (Taberlet 2012). Metabarcoding combines 'meta' — all together — with barcoding, which is species identification using a short genetic marker. The choice of marker determines which organisms are detected, and there is always a trade-off between taxonomic breadth and species-level resolution.
:::


---
